chunk_summary: This section discusses adaptations for therapeutic food stock-outs and visit workflows in outpatient CMAM programs in India, focusing on severe acute malnutrition (SAM) management strategies.
learning_objectives: Understand the challenges of therapeutic food stock-outs in CMAM programs.
Learn strategies to adapt workflows during stock-out periods.
Identify key guidelines for managing SAM in outpatient settings in India.
Explore the role of government and international guidelines in CMAM implementation.
key_facts: India's CMAM programs are guided by the NITI Aayog and Ministry of Health and Family Welfare guidelines.
Therapeutic food stock-outs are a common challenge in SAM management, requiring workflow adaptations.
Operational guidelines emphasize the use of locally available nutrient-dense food alternatives during stock-outs.
Regular follow-up visits are critical to monitor SAM recovery, even during stock-out periods.
The WHO provides global guidelines for the prevention and management of wasting, which are adapted for local contexts.
reference_quote: Operational guidelines emphasize the use of locally available nutrient-dense food alternatives during stock-outs. (Source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, 2011)
document_key_topics: Role of government and WHO in CMAM
